steps.title: Set off the appositive
steps.content: The subject is cephalopods, followed by an appositive that explains what cephalopods are. Because that appositive contains internal commas, matching dashes clearly enclose the full description before the verb have.

steps.title: Reject punctuation errors
steps.content: Choice A opens the appositive but never closes it. Choice B incorrectly splits ocean dwellers from the phrase that modifies it and adds a comma before the verb. Choice C misuses a colon after include.

steps.title: Choose matched dashes
steps.content: The remaining version places the whole appositive between two dashes and keeps cephalopods connected to the verb have. The answer is D.
